name: Ceratozamia
description: [FILL IN] Ceratozamia is a genus of cycads in the family Zamiaceae, native primarily to Mexico with a few species extending into Guatemala and Belize. The genus is distinguished by the paired horn-like processes (hence 'cerato', from the Greek for horn) on the sporophylls of both male and female cones. Most species inhabit cloud forests and moist mountain habitats.
etymology: [FILL IN] From the Greek 'keras' (horn) and 'Zamia', referring to the paired horn-like projections on the cone scales.

conservation_status.notes: [FILL IN] Most species are Endangered or Critically Endangered due to restricted ranges and habitat degradation.

distribution.habitat: [FILL IN] Cloud forests, humid montane forests, and moist ravines; often in shaded or semi-shaded conditions.

morphology.growth_form: [FILL IN] Small to medium cycad; stem may be subterranean or short above ground.
morphology.stem: [FILL IN] Mostly subterranean or short cylindrical trunk.
morphology.leaves: [FILL IN] Pinnately compound; leaflets lanceolate, often with undulate or entire margins; some with golden-bronze new growth.
morphology.reproductive_structures: [FILL IN] Dioecious; cone sporophylls bear distinctive paired horn-like processes. Male cones cylindrical; female cones barrel-shaped.
morphology.seeds_or_spores: [FILL IN] Seeds with fleshy, coloured sarcotesta.
morphology.roots: [FILL IN] Coralloid roots with nitrogen-fixing cyanobacteria.

ecology.pollination: [FILL IN] Insect pollination; beetles recorded as primary pollinators in some species.
ecology.symbioses: [FILL IN] Cyanobacterial nitrogen fixation in coralloid roots.

uses.horticultural: [FILL IN] Grown by collectors; shade-tolerant habit makes some species suited to indoor or shaded garden settings.

cultivation.light: [FILL IN] Partial to full shade; more shade-tolerant than most cycads.
cultivation.watering: [FILL IN] Regular moisture; does not tolerate prolonged drought.
cultivation.notes: [FILL IN] CITES Appendix I or II; all parts toxic.
